title: Walk-Forward Efficiency (WFE) Ratio Validator
summary: Add a Walk-Forward Efficiency (WFE) metric to backtester.py that measures how well in-sample (IS) optimized parameters translate to out-of-sample (OOS) performance. WFE = OOS_annualized_return / IS_annualized_return. A WFE > 0.8 indicates robust parameters; WFE 0.5–0.8 is marginal; WFE < 0.5 signals likely overfitting and the strategy should be discarded or re-specified. IBKR's deep dive on walk-forward analysis (2025) confirms this is the industry-standard check for live deployment readiness. The backtester already computes returns — this feature adds a rolling-window OOS split and a single pass/fail flag before any strategy is promoted to the brain loop.
research_topic: Win Rate Improvement — Backtesting Robustness, Walk-Forward Validation & Intraday Timing Signals
expected_impact: Prevents deploying overfit strategies; research shows >90% of academic quant strategies with positive IS Sharpe produce negative OOS Sharpe — WFE < 0.5 is an early discard flag
